ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Dhokla. Nearest parent is a steamed legume-based cake. A new concept for Gujarati steamed fermented snacks is recommended.
ontology.foodex2_description: Steamed fermented chickpea flour cake, Gujarati (closest parent)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code exists for Dhokla; A03RJ used as closest parent for steamed fermented legume-based preparations.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for Dhokla as a specific food item.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Dhokla is 4 pieces (approximately 150 g), as commonly served at Indian sweet shops and Gujarati restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods — Chickpea flour (besan) and steamed fermented preparation data
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research — National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values for Dhokla derived from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods using chickpea flour (besan) composition data and steamed fermented preparation adjustments. No single ICMR-NIN entry for Dhokla as a composite dish; values represent best-fit adaptation from besan and fermented preparation data. Vitamin B12 (0.05 mcg) reflects minor contribution from yogurt fermentation; this is a low-confidence micronutrient value. Monounsaturated fat and polyunsaturated fat not reported in source; set to -1.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Chickpea Flour and Steamed Chickpea Preparations — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 35 estimated from University of Sydney GI Database entry for steamed chickpea flour preparation and chickpea flour (besan). No direct GI entry for Dhokla exists; the analogous food is steamed chickpea flour cake. Glycemic load per 150 g serving = (35 × 26.5 × 1.5) / 100 = 13.9. Confidence 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content — baking soda (sodium bicarbonate) contribution
hypertension.guidance: Dhokla contains approximately 310 mg sodium per 100 g (465 mg per 150 g serving), primarily from baking soda; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 1–2 pieces and request reduced-salt preparation where possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~35) from chickpea flour and fermentation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Dhokla has a low GI of approximately 35 and a glycemic load of ~14 per 150 g serving, making it a suitable snack for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in standard 4-piece portions without added sugar in the tempering.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat, steamed preparation, high dietary fibre from chickpea flour
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Dhokla is steamed (not fried), contains only 0.5 g saturated fat and 3.2 g dietary fibre per 100 g, and provides folate (52 mcg) and potassium (215 mg), making it a heart-friendly snack; sodium intake should be monitored in those with concurrent hypertension.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Dhokla is a beloved Gujarati snack from the state of Gujarat in western India — a spongy, light, soft steamed cake made from a fermented batter of chickpea flour (besan) and yogurt, leavened with baking soda or Eno fruit salt. After steaming in a shallow tray, it is cut into diamond or square shapes and finished with a hot oil tempering (tadka) of mustard seeds, curry leaves, and green chilli, then garnished with grated coconut and fresh coriander. The flavour is pleasantly tangy from the yogurt and lemon, mildly sweet from the batter, and fragrant from the tempering. Dhokla is gluten-free, low in fat (steamed, not fried), and has a low glycemic index of approximately 35, making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. It provides 155 kcal, 6.5 g protein, 3.2 g fat, and 26.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g, along with 3.2 g dietary fibre and 52 mcg folate. Sodium is moderate at 310 mg per 100 g due to baking soda. Available at Indian sweet shops and Gujarati restaurants across Singapore.
common_user_queries: dhokla vs idli which is healthier

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN chickpea flour and steamed fermented preparation data; no single ICMR-NIN entry exists for Dhokla as a composite dish. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for steamed chickpea flour preparations. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the composite-dish adaptation.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for Dhokla (steamed fermented chickpea flour cake) is not available in ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods as of 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this composite steamed preparation is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source consulted. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — steamed preparation with no hydrogenated fats). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — plant-based fermented preparation with no animal fat or fortification). All core macros (protein_g 6.5, fat_g 3.2, carbohydrate_g 26.5) are non-zero and sourced from ICMR-NIN adapted data. FoodEx2 code A03RJ used as closest parent; no exact code exists for Dhokla. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for steamed chickpea flour preparations.

improvement_actions_taken: Added Hindi local name 'ढोकला' to local_names.hindi and aliases.
Added 'savoury chickpea cake', 'Gujarati dhokla', 'besan dhokla' to English aliases for richer retrieval coverage.
Populated aliases_by_language with all language groups and verified consistency with flat aliases array.
Expanded natural_language_description to exceed 200 characters with clinical and nutritional detail.
Added cardiovascular_disease health context condition (was absent in input).
Renamed 'diabetes' condition to 'type2_diabetes' with human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' per scoring rules.
Added monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, water_g as -1 with full documentation in notes.
Documented trans_fat_g 0.0 and vitamin_d_mcg 0.0 as confirmed zero in notes.
Populated gi_source as structured object per RESULT template.
Set confidence to 'medium' with justification reflecting composite-dish adaptation.
